sections.label: Section 1378
sections.original: οὐ δῆτ ’, ἐπεί σφας τῇδ ’ ἐγὼ θάψω χερί ,
sections.translation: No , never ! I will bury them myself , bearing them to Hera ’ s sacred field , who watches o ’ er the Cape ,

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: ὡς μή τις αὐτοὺς πολεμίων καθυβρίσῃ ,
sections.translation: that none of their foes may insult them by pulling down their tombs ; and in this land of Sisyphus I will ordain hereafter a solemn feast and mystic rites to atone for this impious murder . Myself will now to the land of Erechtheus ,

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.translation: to dwell with Aegeus , Pandion ’ s son . But thou , as well thou mayest , shalt die a caitiff ’ s death , thy head [*] crushed ’ neath a shattered relic of Argo , when thou hast seen the bitter ending of my marriage .

sections.label: Section 1389
sections.translation: The curse of our sons ’ avenging spirit and of Justice ,

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.translation: that calls for blood , be on thee !

sections.label: Section 1391
sections.original: τίς δὲ κλύει σοῦ θεὸς ἢ δαίμων ,
sections.translation: What god or power divine hears thee , breaker of oaths and every law of hospitality ?

sections.label: Section 1393
sections.translation: Fie upon thee ! cursed witch ! child-murderess !

sections.label: Section 1394
sections.original: στεῖχε πρὸς οἴκους καὶ θάπτ ’ ἄλοχον .
sections.translation: To thy house ! go , bury thy wife .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.translation: I go , bereft of both my sons .

sections.label: Section 1396
sections.translation: Thy grief is yet to come ; wait till old age is with thee too .

sections.label: Section 1397
sections.translation: O my dear , dear children !

sections.label: Section 1397b
sections.translation: Dear to their mother , not to thee .

sections.label: Section 1398
sections.translation: And yet thou didst slay them ?

sections.label: Section 1398b
sections.translation: Yea , to vex thy heart .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.translation: One last fond kiss , ah me !

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.translation: I fain would on their lips imprint .

sections.label: Section 1401
sections.translation: Embraces now , and fond farewells for them ; but then a cold repulse !

sections.label: Section 1402b
sections.translation: By heaven I do adjure thee , let me touch their tender skin .

sections.label: Section 1404
sections.translation: No , no ! in vain this word has sped its flight .

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.translation: O Zeus , dost hear how I am driven hence ; dost mark the treatment I receive from this she-lion , fell murderess of her young ? Yet so far as I may and can , I raise for them a dirge ,

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.translation: and do adjure [*] the gods to witness how thou hast slain my sons , and wilt not suffer me to embrace or bury their dead bodies . Would I had never begotten them to see thee slay them after all !

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.translation: Many a fate doth Zeus dispense , high on his Olympian throne ; oft do the gods bring things to pass beyond man ’ s expectation ; that , which we thought would be , is not fulfilled , while for the unlooked-for god finds out a way ; and such hath been the issue of this matter .
